definition:
βέβηλος, -ον 
 (βαίνω, whence βηλός, a threshold), [in LXX chiefly for חֹל ;] 
   1. permitted to be trodden, accessible (see DCG, ii, 422b); hence, 
   2. (opposite to ἱερός) unhallowed, profane: of things, 1Ti.4:7 6:20, 2Ti.2:16; of men, 1Ti.1:9, Heb.12:16 (cf. κοινός; Cremer, 140).
† (AS)
